sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: The proteolysis-targeting chimera (PROTAC) landscape is experiencing significant momentum, marked by regulatory milestones and evolving clinical strategies within oncology. Arvinas recently secured a landmark approval for its PROTAC protein degrader, signaling a pivotal shift in therapeutic modalities despite clinical benefits being narrower than initially projected. Concurrently, expert discourse at the 3rd Biennial Miami Precision Medicine Conference highlighted the integration of novel degraders into lymphoma care, specifically targeting BCL2, BCL6, BIRD-2, and BTK proteins. Craig H. Moskowitz, MD, noted that these degraders are approaching approval and represent a move away from traditional cytotoxic regimens and autologous stem cell transplantation in conditions like mantle cell lymphoma and diffuse large B-cell lymphoma. The field is transitioning toward molecularly defined interventions where PROTACs function alongside T-cell engagers and CAR T-cell therapy to de-escalate therapy while maintaining efficacy. This convergence of regulatory approval and clinical adoption underscores the growing validation of protein degradation as a viable mechanism for treating hematologic malignancies, positioning PROTACs as critical components in the future of precision oncology. Furthermore, the rise of these novel agents suggests a future where all lymphoma patients are sequenced upfront to define therapy based on tumor sequence, reducing the reliance on high-dose therapy.

points.text: Arvinas — PROTAC protein degrader — Oncology — Landmark FDA approval secured with narrower-than-expected clinical benefit noted.
BTK, BCL2, BCL6, BIRD-2 degraders — Protein degradation — Lymphoma (MCL/DLBCL) — Emerging therapeutic class; BTK degraders closest to approval.
Arvinas: Secured landmark approval for PROTAC protein degrader, driving R&D momentum in the field.

PROTACs — Degrader mechanism — Mantle Cell Lymphoma (MCL) and Diffuse Large B-cell Lymphoma (DLBCL) — Targeting BCL2, BCL6, BIRD-2, and BTK proteins; contributing to decline of autologous stem cell transplantation.
Arvinas PROTAC — Protein degrader — Oncology — Approval granted with narrower-than-expected clinical benefit noted.
BTK degraders — Degrader mechanism — Lymphoma — Closest to approval among targeted agents.
